steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The key signal is "extremely influential" — the sentence after the blank must show what that influence actually produced.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is specification. Sentence 1 makes a broad claim — the research was "extremely influential"; Sentence 2 names the concrete outcome that proves it. The blank needs a transition signaling that Sentence 2 provides a specific instance of the claimed influence.
  • Choice A — ‘Specifically,’: signals that Sentence 2 gives a concrete detail supporting the general claim — the treaty is the specific proof of the research's influence. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Regardless,’: signals that Sentence 2 proceeds despite something — there is nothing to dismiss or work around here. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Earlier,’: signals a time earlier than Sentence 1 — but the 1987 treaty came after the 1974 research, not before. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘However,’: signals contrast — Sentence 2 is the expected result of influential research, not a contradiction of it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
